summary:
You are given an array of points on a 2D plane, where points[i] = [xi, yi]. The cost of connecting two points is their Manhattan distance: |xi - xj| + |yi - yj|.

Return the minimum total cost to connect every point so that there is exactly one path between any two of them. In other words, build a minimum spanning tree over the points and report its total weight.

examples.explanation: Connecting the points with the cheapest set of edges that links all five together costs 20 in total Manhattan distance.
The two edges (-4,1)-(-2,5) of cost 6 and (-2,5)-(3,12) of cost 12 connect all three points for a total of 18.

constraints: Edge cost between two points is the Manhattan distance |xi - xj| + |yi - yj|
